description: Eleven cooperating skills that implement the loss-function-driven (LFD) agentic-loop pattern: turn a high-level goal into a /goal prompt, scaffold a harness, and run the outer loop until the inner agent's skill converges. The bundle ships with six agent-adapter skills (Cline, Claude Code, Codex, Hermes Agent, OpenCode, fake-agent) so the same loop works across coding agents and across deterministic dogfood testing.

skills.name: loss-function-design
skills.summary: The 4-piece loss anatomy (target / constraints / instruments / forced entropy). The other 10 skills reference this vocabulary.
skills.load_trigger: user says 'loss function', 'reward hacking', 'verifier', 'gate sub-loss', or describes a stuck loop
skills.description: How to design, implement, validate, and iterate loss functions for agentic development loops. The loss function is the objective the loop is optimizing against — without a good one, the loop spirals o

skills.name: harness-engineering
skills.summary: What the agent sees: context, tools, scaffolding, observability. The agent-facing half of the LFD pattern.
skills.load_trigger: user says 'harness', 'agent context', 'AGENTS.md layout', or designs a new project's repo structure
skills.description: The "harness engineering" playbook, distilled from Ryan Lopopolo's OpenAI post ("Harness engineering: leveraging Codex in an agent-first world", 2026-02-11) and from the @elvissun X post + diagram abo

skills.name: cline-orchestration
skills.substitute: any other agent-adapter skill in this bundle (claude-code, codex, hermes-agent, opencode, fake-agent) if not using Cline
skills.summary: Cline v3 CLI surface, NDJSON parser, wrapper contract, per-iteration isolation.
skills.load_trigger: user says 'Cline', 'Cline version', 'wrapper script', 'Cline flags', or the wrapper errors out
skills.description: How to drive the Cline CLI as the coding agent inside a loss-function-driven loop. Cline is the *tool surface* the agent uses inside the loop. The driver (Hermes or any other orchestrator) owns the lo

skills.substitute: any other agent-adapter skill in this bundle (cline, codex, hermes-agent, opencode, fake-agent) if not using Claude Code
skills.summary: Claude Code CLI (Anthropic) print-mode surface, --output-format json parser, wrapper contract, per-iteration isolation.
skills.load_trigger: user says 'Claude Code', 'claude -p', 'Claude Code wrapper', or the Claude Code wrapper errors out
skills.description: How to drive the Claude Code CLI (Anthropic) as the coding agent inside a loss-function-driven loop. The driver (Hermes or any orchestrator) owns the loop, the loss function, and the verifier runs. Cl

skills.substitute: any other agent-adapter skill in this bundle (cline, claude-code, hermes-agent, opencode, fake-agent) if not using Codex
skills.summary: OpenAI Codex CLI exec-mode surface, --json NDJSON parser, wrapper contract, git-init per-iteration isolation.
skills.load_trigger: user says 'Codex', 'codex exec', 'Codex wrapper', or the Codex wrapper errors out
skills.description: How to drive the OpenAI Codex CLI as the coding agent inside a loss-function-driven loop. The driver (Hermes or any orchestrator) owns the loop, the loss function, and the verifier runs. Codex owns th

skills.name: hermes-agent-orchestration
skills.substitute: any other agent-adapter skill in this bundle (cline, claude-code, codex, opencode, fake-agent) if not using Hermes Agent
skills.summary: Hermes Agent CLI chat-mode surface, --output-format json parser, wrapper contract, profile-based per-iteration isolation.

skills.description: How to drive the Hermes Agent CLI (Nous Research) as the coding agent inside a loss-function-driven loop. The driver (Hermes or any orchestrator) owns the outer loop, the loss function, and the verifi

skills.name: opencode-orchestration
skills.substitute: any other agent-adapter skill in this bundle (cline, claude-code, codex, hermes-agent, fake-agent) if not using OpenCode
skills.summary: OpenCode CLI run-mode surface, --format json parser, wrapper contract, --workdir per-iteration isolation.
skills.load_trigger: user says 'OpenCode', 'opencode run', 'OpenCode wrapper', or the OpenCode wrapper errors out
skills.description: How to drive the OpenCode CLI (opencode.ai) as the coding agent inside a loss-function-driven loop. The driver (Hermes or any orchestrator) owns the loop, the loss function, and the verifier runs. Ope

skills.name: fake-agent-orchestration
skills.substitute: any other agent-adapter skill in this bundle (cline, claude-code, codex, hermes-agent, opencode) if not using the deterministic fake stub
skills.summary: Deterministic stub adapter for dogfood testing. No model, no network, bit-exact output. Used by the LFD system verifier.
skills.load_trigger: user says 'fake agent', 'deterministic test', 'LFD system verifier', or is building a CI test that runs the LFD loop without a real model
skills.description: How to drive the **fake-agent** stub as the inner coding agent inside a loss-function-driven loop. The fake agent is a deterministic stub: it echoes the cycle prompt back as the "candidate text", writ

skills.name: meta-loss-function-development
skills.summary: Turns a high-level goal into a complete harness + paste-able /goal prompt. Loads first, emits the spec that the other skills consume.
skills.load_trigger: user says 'loss function development', 'LFD', '/goal', 'use the loop pattern on X', or describes a project they want to drive with a loss-function loop
skills.description: Turn a high-level goal ("build X in Y using loss-function development") into a COMPLETE, on-disk harness project, then emit a paste-able /goal prompt that runs the outer loop against it. The harness i

skills.name: harness-scaffold
skills.summary: Scaffolds the project tree (verifiers/, instruments/, AGENTS.md, GOAL.md, design + held-out task stubs) from a /goal prompt. Stubs carry a 'TODO: meta-fill before emitting /goal prompt' marker that loop-driver refuses to run on.
skills.load_trigger: user pastes a /goal prompt and says 'scaffold', 'set up the harness', or describes a fresh project root
skills.description: Given a /goal prompt (from meta-loss-function-development) and a project root, scaffold the harness tree: verifiers/, instruments/, design tasks with deterministic graders, AGENTS.md, GOAL.md, README.

skills.name: loop-driver
skills.summary: Runs the outer loop. Reads the iteration log, forms a hypothesis, invokes the inner agent via the wrapper, runs the design set, scores, applies forced entropy, stops on plateau/budget. Refuses to run on incomplete harnesses (stub markers or empty held-out dirs).
skills.load_trigger: user has a scaffolded project and says 'run the loop', 'drive the cycle', 'iterate on the skill'
skills.description: Run the outer loop of a loss-function-driven agentic cycle: read iteration log, write candidate artifact, install it for the agent, run design set, score, log iteration, apply forced entropy on stall,

user_flow: 1. User describes a goal in plain language.
3. User opens a fresh session and pastes the /goal prompt.
4. Fresh session runs harness-scaffold to build the project tree (with --runtime {cline,claude-code,codex,hermes-agent,opencode,fake}).
5. User (or fresh session) fills in the per-task grade.sh scripts.
6. Fresh session runs loop-driver/run-loop.sh to drive the cycles.
7. Loop stops on success / wall-clock / tokens / plateau.

compatibility.cline-orchestration: targets Cline v3.0.34+; the wrapper is portable across Cline versions but the verify-gate reference is Cline-3 specific
compatibility.claude-code-orchestration: targets Claude Code v2.x; the wrapper is portable across v2.x patch versions
compatibility.codex-orchestration: targets Codex CLI v1.x; the wrapper requires git in the iteration dir
compatibility.hermes-agent-orchestration: targets Hermes Agent v2.x; the wrapper is provider-agnostic
compatibility.opencode-orchestration: targets OpenCode v1.x; the wrapper is provider-agnostic
compatibility.fake-agent-orchestration: deterministic stub; no version target, no real CLI

agent_adapters.codex: codex-orchestration (v1.0.0, included) — Codex CLI v1.x (OpenAI-only, requires git)
agent_adapters.fake: fake-agent-orchestration (v1.0.0, included) — deterministic stub for dogfood testing, no model, no network
